Registry / Terms / software separation

software separation

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00356 · 10 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 6 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

6 distinct definitions across 10 publications. 2 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

4 identical2010202X
unambiguous separation of software into legally relevant software and non-legally relevant software. If no software separation exists, the whole software is to be considered as legally relevant
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency
20102006OIML R 51-1:2006T.2.7.8.600597pending
20102006OIML R 76-1:2006T.2.8.700938pending
202X2006OIML R 51-1:2006T.2.7.8.600597pending
202X2006OIML R 76-1:2006T.2.8.700938pending
2 identical2010202X
software in measuring devices can be divided into a legally relevant part and a legally irrelevant part. These parts communicate via an interface
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency
20102007OIML R 21:20072.2.8.600356pending
202X2007OIML R 21:20072.2.8.600356pending

4 unique definitions (each used by only one publication)

Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Definition
202X2023OIML D031:20233.2.6303524
separation of software in measuring instruments, which can be divided into a legally relevant module(s) and legally non-relevant module(s) adapted form [OIML V 1:2022, 6.02]
pending
202X2020OIML R150-1:20202.2.9.603525
separation of software in measuring instruments, which can be divided into a legally relevant part and a legally non-relevant part [VIML:2013, 6.02]
pending
20102008OIML D 31:20083.1.4602213
software in measuring instruments/electronic devices/sub- assemblies can be divided into a legally relevant part and a legally non-relevant part. These parts communicate via a software interface
pending
202X2011OIML R106-1:20110.2.8.602558
unambiguous separation of software into legally relevant software and non-legally relevant software
pending
